title: alchemilla vulgaris proles minor
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris proles minor, also known as Lady's Mantle, is a perennial herb native to Europe, North America, and Asia. It is characterized by its rosettes of glossy leaves and its distinctive, spiky flowers, which are often used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Lady's Mantle include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, digestive issues, and menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, Lady's Mantle is often used as a tea, tincture, or salve to promote healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate menstrual symptoms, and is also valued for its antioxidant and antispasmodic properties, which support overall health and well-being.
